name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Whoopies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Grease baking sheets with Golden Crisco Shortening.
2. In a large bowl, cream the Golden Crisco Shortening, sugar, Egg, and vanilla extract until light and creamy.
3. Gradually add the flour mixture to the creamed mixture, alternating with buttermilk, until smooth.
4. Drop dough by heaping teaspoonfuls onto greased baking sheets.
5. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 7 to 9 minutes, or until set. Remove cookies to a cooling rack and cool completely.
Filling:
6. In a small saucepan, combine milk and flour. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened. Cover surface with plastic wrap; let cool completely.
7. Beat the cooled milk mixture, Golden Crisco Shortening, peanut butter, and vanilla extract together until smooth. Gradually add icing sugar, beating on low speed until blended. Beat on high speed until smooth and creamy. Chill until ready to use.
8. Spread filling onto the flat side of half the cookies. Place remaining cookies rounded side up on top and gently press together.
9. If desired, roll the edges of the cookie in peanut butter chips and gently press into the filling to stick.
Makes: about 3 dozen cookies
Preparation time: 35 minutes
Baking time: 9 minutes
Ice cream sandwiches: Replace filling with a generous amount of your favorite ice cream. If desired, roll in chips. Wrap and freeze until ready to use.
